2603.23938.title: OmniACBench: A Benchmark for Evaluating Context-Grounded Acoustic Control in Omni-Modal Models
2603.23938.paper_summary: Most testbeds for omni-modal models assess multimodal understanding via textual outputs, leaving it unclear whether these models can properly speak their answers. To study this, we introduce OmniACBench, a benchmark for evaluating context-grounded acoustic control in omni-modal models. Given a spoken instruction, a text script, and an image, a model must read the script aloud with an appropriate tone and manner. OmniACBench comprises 3,559 verified instances covering six acoustic features: speech rate, phonation, pronunciation, emotion, global accent, and timbre. Extensive experiments on eight models reveal their limitations in the proposed setting, despite their strong performance on prior textual-output evaluations. Our analyses show that the main bottleneck lies not in processing individual modalities, but in integrating multimodal context for faithful speech generation. Moreover, we identify three common failure modes-weak direct control, failed implicit inference, and failed multimodal grounding-providing insights for developing models that can verbalize responses effectively.
2603.23938.paper_summary_zh: 大多數全模態模型的測試平台通過文本輸出來評估多模態理解，這使得這些模型是否能夠正確地口頭表達答案變得不明確。為了研究這一點，我們引入了 OmniACBench，這是一個用於評估全模態模型中基於上下文的聲音控制的基準。給定一個口語指令、一個文本腳本和一張圖片，模型必須以適當的語調和方式朗讀腳本。OmniACBench 包含 3,559 個經過驗證的實例，涵蓋六種聲學特徵：語速、發聲、發音、情感、全球口音和音色。對八個模型進行的廣泛實驗揭示了它們在所提出的設置中的局限性，儘管它們在先前的文本輸出評估中表現強勁。我們的分析顯示，主要瓶頸不在於處理單一模態，而在於整合多模態上下文以實現忠實的語音生成。此外，我們確定了三種常見的失敗模式——弱直接控制、隱性推理失敗和多模態基礎失敗——為開發能夠有效口頭表達回應的模型提供了見解。
2603.23938.author: Seunghee Kim et.al.
2603.23938.authors: Seunghee Kim, Bumkyu Park, Kyudan Jung, Joosung Lee, Soyoon Kim, Jeonghoon Kim, Taeuk Kim, Hwiyeol Jo
